title: Recovery private key bytes not zeroized after use
description:
In `cmd_persona Create` (cli.rs:503-535) and `cmd_did Backfill` (cli.rs:1062-1081), the recovery private key `recovery_priv: [u8; 32]` and the mnemonic string `recovery_phrase: String` are left on the stack/heap until natural drop. No explicit zeroization occurs. The `zeroize` crate is not in dependencies.

While Rust's drop semantics mean the memory is freed, it is not zeroed — the key material persists in memory (and potentially on disk via swap) until overwritten by other allocations. For a recovery key that grants full account control, this is a meaningful residual risk.

The `zeroize` crate (from RustCrypto) is the standard fix. `ed25519-dalek` already supports `Zeroize` on `SigningKey`. The stack array and String should use `Zeroizing<>` wrappers.

Affected: src/cli.rs:503-535, src/cli.rs:1062-1081, src/did.rs:4-11 (generate_recovery_keypair returns raw bytes)
close_reason: Deferred: requires zeroize crate, addressed in key storage design doc
